was the 23rd season of [Major League Soccer](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer"), top division of soccer in the United States and Canada. The regular season began on March 3, 2018 and concluded on October 28, 2018. The [MLS Cup Playoffs](/wiki/2018_MLS_Cup_Playoffs "2018 MLS Cup Playoffs") began on October 31, 2018 and concluded with [MLS Cup 2018](/wiki/MLS_Cup_2018 "MLS Cup 2018") on December 8, 2018. The league took a nine-day hiatus in early June for the [2018 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2018_FIFA_World_Cup "2018 FIFA World Cup"), reduced from previous breaks.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The **2018 Major League Soccer season** was the 23rd season of [Major League Soccer](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer"), top division of soccer in the United States and Canada. The regular season began on March 3, 2018 and concluded on October 28, 2018. The [MLS Cup Playoffs](/wiki/2018_MLS_Cup_Playoffs "2018 MLS Cup Playoffs") began on October 31, 2018 and concluded with [MLS Cup 2018](/wiki/MLS_Cup_2018 "MLS Cup 2018") on December 8, 2018. The league took a nine-day hiatus in early June for the [2018 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2018_FIFA_World_Cup "2018 FIFA World Cup"), reduced from previous breaks.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[Los Angeles FC](/wiki/Los_Angeles_FC "Los Angeles FC") joined the league as an expansion franchise, while [D.C. United](/wiki/D.C._United "D.C. United") debuted their new [soccer-specific stadium](/wiki/Soccer-specific_stadium "Soccer-specific stadium"), [Audi Field](/wiki/Audi_Field "Audi Field").

[Toronto FC](/wiki/Toronto_FC "Toronto FC") were the defending [Supporters' Shield](/wiki/Supporters%27_Shield "Supporters' Shield") champions and defending [MLS Cup](/wiki/MLS_Cup "MLS Cup") champions.

[New York Red Bulls](/wiki/New_York_Red_Bulls "New York Red Bulls") won their third Supporters' Shield, with a league record 71 points, while [Atlanta United FC](/wiki/Atlanta_United_FC "Atlanta United FC") won their first MLS Cup in their second year in the league.

## Regular season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_Major_League_Soccer_season&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regular season")]

### Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_Major_League_Soccer_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Format")]

During the 2018 MLS regular season, each team played 34 games, including 17 home games and 17 away games. Teams faced each of their conference opponents (10 in the East, 11 in the West) twice during the season with one game at home and one game away. Western Conference teams played one additional intra-conference game and Eastern Conference teams played two. All teams faced each non-conference opponent once.

### SuperDraft

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_Major_League_Soccer_season&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: SuperDraft")]

Further information: [2018 MLS SuperDraft](/wiki/2018_MLS_SuperDraft "2018 MLS SuperDraft")

The [MLS SuperDraft](/wiki/MLS_SuperDraft "MLS SuperDraft") is an annual event, taking place in January of each year, in which the teams of Major League Soccer select players who have graduated from college or otherwise been signed by the league. The first two rounds of [2018 MLS SuperDraft](/wiki/2018_MLS_SuperDraft "2018 MLS SuperDraft") were held on January 19 in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia"). Rounds three and four of the 2018 SuperDraft were held via conference call on January 21. [Los Angeles FC](/wiki/Los_Angeles_FC "Los Angeles FC") selected [João Moutinho](/wiki/Jo%C3%A3o_Moutinho_(footballer,_born_1998) "João Moutinho (footballer, born 1998)") with the first overall pick.

### Allocation ranking

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_Major_League_Soccer_season&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Allocation ranking")]

The allocation ranking is the mechanism used to determine which MLS club has first priority to acquire a player who is in the MLS allocation list. The MLS allocation list contains select [U.S. National Team](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_soccer_team "United States men's national soccer team") players and players transferred outside of MLS garnering a transfer fee of at least $500,000. The allocations are ranked in reverse order of finish for the 2017 season, taking playoff performance into account.[[116]](#cite_note-122) As an expansion team, Los Angeles FC took the top spot.

Once the club uses its allocation ranking to acquire a player, it drops to the bottom of the list. A ranking can be traded provided that part of the compensation received in return is another club's ranking. At all times each club is assigned one ranking. The rankings reset at the end of each MLS season.
